name: qt6-main

about.description:
Qt helps you create connected devices, UIs & applications that run
anywhere on any device, on any operating system at any time.
about.summary: Qt is a cross-platform application and UI framework.

about.description:
Qt helps you create connected devices, UIs & applications that run

about.summary: Qt is a cross-platform application and UI framework.

raw_meta_yaml:
context:
  version: "6.11.0"
  maj_min: ${{ (version | split('.'))[:2] | join('.') }}

package:
  name: qt6-main
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  - url: https://ftp.fau.de/qtproject/official_releases/qt/${{ maj_min }}/${{ version }}/single/qt-everywhere-src-${{ version }}.tar.xz
    md5: a93e9f424a9d11ee8d67bf8fb1af4772
    patches:
      # qtbase
      - patches/0003-qtbase-use-better-clang-optimize-size.patch

      # dont look for qt.conf in bundle directory
      - patches/0015-qt6-conf-osx.patch

      # Ensure that cross-compiled builds and native builds generated the same cmake config files
      - patches/0020-remove-qt-host-path-check-in-cross-compiled-builds.patch

      # Ensure that QLibraryInfo::path(QLibraryInfo::PrefixPath) returns the correct value on Windows
      # after .dll are copied from %CONDA_PREFIX%\Library\lib\qt6\bin to %CONDA_PREFIX%\bin
      # Fix for https://github.com/conda-forge/qt-main-feedstock/issues/275
      # Never apply this to other operating systems, as there the libraries are not manually moved
      - patches/0025-fix-qlibraryinfo-prefixpath-windows.patch

      # qmake fails configuring qwt on osx: Could not find qmake spec 'macx-clang'
      - patches/0034-specpath-macos.patch

  - url: https://download.qt.io/development_releases/prebuilt/llvmpipe/windows/opengl32sw-64-mesa_12_0_rc2.7z
    sha256: 2a0d2f92c60e0962ef5f6039d3793424c6f39e49ba27ac04a5b21ca4ae012e15
    target_directory: opengl32sw

build:
  number: 0
  # bumping qt version requires a 2-staged build as cross builds require the native package with the same version
  skip: ${{ build_platform != target_platform }}

requirements:
  build:
    - ${{ compiler('c') }}
    - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
    - cmake
    - ninja
    - curl
    - perl
    - if: unix
      then:
        # For fontconfig + freetype
        - pkg-config
        - make
    - if: linux
      then:
        - ruby >=2.5
        - bison
        - flex
        - gperf   
    - if: win
      then:
        - m2-gperf
        - m2-bison
        - m2-flex
        - m2-patch
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then:
        # libclang is required in order to enable simultaneous x86_64 and
        # arm64 support for QDoc
        - libclang
        # Although these should be host-only dependencies, they are needed when
        # cross-compiling arm64 binaries on x86_64 since llvm-config (host/arm64)
        # cannot be executed on x86_64 to locate libclang successfully
        - llvmdev
        - clangdev >=9
        # circular dependency... but it helps when cross compiling
        - qt6-main ==${{ version }}
        # explicit libxml2+icu
        - libxml2-devel
        - icu

  host:
    - brotli
    - harfbuzz
    - glib
    - icu
    - libjpeg-turbo
    - libpng
    - libsqlite
    - pcre2
    - zlib
    - openssl
    # For QDoc
    # http://doc.qt.io/Qt-5/qdoc-guide-clang.html
    # Only libclang is needed, but needs the full package for detection.
    - llvm
    - clangdev >=9
    # Ensure that build and host have the same clang version on osx
    # otherwise qt6 may be out of sync compared to the pinned compiler
    # version
    - zstd
    - krb5
    - double-conversion
    - libtiff
    - libwebp
    - if: osx
      then:
        - libclang ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
        - libclang-cpp ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
    - if: not osx
      then:
        - libclang
        - libclang-cpp
        - libvulkan-headers
        - libvulkan-loader
        - freetype
    - if: not win
      then:
        # We are deciding not to depend on mysql-devel
        # to keep this package obviously LGPL licensed
        # as opposed to "maybe GPL"
        # https://github.com/conda-forge/qt-main-feedstock/issues/335
        # - mysql-devel
        - postgresql
    - if: linux
      then:
        # For pkg-config gl, fontconfig + freetype
        - pthread-stubs
        - libdrm
        - libgl-devel
        - libegl-devel
        - dbus
        - fontconfig
        - libxml2-devel
        - libxkbcommon
        - libxcb
        - libdrm
        - libcups
        - alsa-lib
        - wayland
        # The xcb-util packages are necessary to build the Qt xcb plugin.
        # It was decided to not use CDTs for qt to resolve issues
        # that may exhibit as segfault due to missing libraries
        # in some lighter weight linux installations
        # https://github.com/conda-forge/qt-main-feedstock/issues/32
        - xcb-util
        - xcb-util-wm
        - xcb-util-keysyms
        - xcb-util-image
        - xcb-util-renderutil
        - xcb-util-cursor
        - xorg-libx11
        - xorg-libxext
        - xorg-libxdamage
        - xorg-libxrandr
        - xorg-libxcomposite
        - xorg-libxcursor
        - xorg-libsm
        - xorg-libice
        # https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/pull/22084
        - xorg-libxxf86vm
        - xorg-libxtst
        - xorg-xorgproto
  run:
    - if: linux
      then:
        - libxcb
        # Adding the following xcb-util run requirements resolves issue
        # that may exhibit as segfault due to missing libraries
        # in some lighter weight linux installations
        # https://github.com/conda-forge/qt-main-feedstock/issues/32
        - xcb-util
        - xcb-util-wm
        - xcb-util-keysyms
        - xcb-util-image
        - xcb-util-renderutil
        - xcb-util-cursor
  run_constraints:
    - qt ==${{ version }}
  run_exports:
    - ${{ pin_subpackage('qt6-main', upper_bound='x.x') }}

tests:
  - script:
      - qmake6 --version
      - if: unix
        then:
          - test -d $PREFIX/include/qt6/QtCore
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Core${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Gui${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Network${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6OpenGL${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6OpenGLWidgets${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6PrintSupport${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6ShaderTools${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Svg${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Test${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Qml${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Quick${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Widgets${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6Xml${SHLIB_EXT}
        else:
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\include\\qt6\\QtCore exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Core.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\bin\\Qt6Core.dll exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Gui.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Network.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6OpenGL.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6OpenGLWidgets.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6PrintSupport.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6ShaderTools.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Svg.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Test.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Qml.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Quick.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Widgets.lib exit 1
          - if not exist %PREFIX%\\Library\\lib\\Qt6Xml.lib exit 1
      - if: linux
        then:
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/qt6/plugins/platforms/libqxcb.so
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/qt6/plugins/platforms/libqeglfs.so
      - if: unix
        then:
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/qt6/plugins/sqldrivers/libqsqlite${SHLIB_EXT}
          - test -f $PREFIX/lib/qt6/plugins/imageformats/libqtiff${SHLIB_EXT}
          # We needed to add a related flag, but these two SO files should be part of the
          # qt6-wayland conda package
          - test ! -f $PREFIX/lib/libQt6WaylandCompositor${SHLIB_EXT}
    requirements:
      run:
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
        - cmake >=3.16
        - if: unix
          then: make
        - if: linux
          then:
            - libgl-devel
            - libegl-devel
            - xcb-util
            - xorg-libxdamage
            # https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/pull/22084
            - xorg-libxxf86vm
    files:
      recipe:
        - test/main-qtwebengine.cpp
        - test/main.cpp
        - test/main.qml
        - test/qml.qrc
        - test/qrc_qml.cpp
        - test/test_qmimedatabase.cpp
        - test/test_qlibraryinfo_prefixpath.cpp
        - test/CMakeLists.txt
        - test/main.pro

about:
  homepage: https://qt.io
  license: LGPL-3.0-only
  license_file: LICENSES/LGPL-3.0-only.txt
  summary: 'Qt is a cross-platform application and UI framework.'
  description: |
    Qt helps you create connected devices, UIs & applications that run
    anywhere on any device, on any operating system at any time.
  documentation: https://doc.qt.io/
  repository: https://github.com/qt

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - traversaro
    - andfoy
    - ccordoba12
    - gillins
    - mingwandroid
    - msarahan
    - ocefpaf
    - stuarteberg
    - Tobias-Fischer
    - matthiasdiener
    - jschueller

about.description:
Qt helps you create connected devices, UIs & applications that run
anywhere on any device, on any operating system at any time.
